name: Old town of Rodén from Fuentes de Ebro
name_original: Antiguo pueblo de Rodén desde Fuentes de Ebro
region_intro: Hiking around Fuentes De Ebro offers diverse landscapes within the Ebro Depression in Aragon, Spain. The region features extensive gypsum and alabaster deposits, creating a unique semi-arid terrain with active and former quarries. Trails often provide views of the Ebro river, its fertile plain, and surrounding agricultural areas. Green spaces with Aleppo pine forests and repopulated areas with wild olive and juniper also contribute to the varied environment. Best hiking trails around Fuentes De Ebro The most popular hiking route is Toro de Osborne and Castillo de Cornel - circular through the Montes Blancos de Alfajarín, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the Montes Blancos de Alfajarín. Another top favourite among local hikers is Old town of Rodén from Fuentes de Ebro, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This trail leads to the historic old town of Rodén, offering cultural insights. Local hikers also love the Presa de Pina from El Burgo del Ebro — circular via the Ebro Natural Path, an 8.6 miles (13.9 km) trail leading through the Ebro Natural Path, often completed in about 3 hours 31 minutes. Hiking around Fuentes De Ebro is defined by river plains, alabaster quarries, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Fuentes De Ebro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Fuentes De Ebro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Fuentes De Ebro?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes around Fuentes De Ebro, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy walks to more challenging trails.

Q: What kind of terrain and natural features can I expect to see on the hikes?
A: The region is characterized by its unique geology, including extensive gypsum and alabaster deposits, which create a semi-arid landscape. Many trails offer panoramic views of the Ebro river, its fertile plain, and surrounding agricultural areas. You'll also encounter green spaces with Aleppo pine forests and repopulated areas featuring wild olive and juniper.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Fuentes De Ebro?
A: Yes, Fuentes De Ebro offers several easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979614617/toro-de-osborne-and-castillo-de-cornel-circular-through-the-montes-blancos-de-alfajarin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toro de Osborne and Castillo de Cornel - circular through the Montes Blancos de Alfajarín&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4-mile (6.4 km) trail that explores the Montes Blancos de Alfajarín, perfect for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on all trails are not detailed, generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in Spain, especially if kept on a leash. Always ensure your dog is under control and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes to enjoy. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980034722/bird-reserve-of-el-planeron-circular-tour-around-the-steppes-of-belchite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bird Reserve of El Planeron — circular tour around the steppes of Belchite&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.3-mile (8.5 km) trail offering a comprehensive experience of the steppes.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I discover along the trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, you can encounter historical sites. The old town of Rodén, a nearby hamlet, features Islamic ruins and buildings constructed with alabaster, bearing witness to the Spanish Civil War. Some routes also offer views of Civil War trenches and the modern AVE bridge. You can also explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cabezo de la Horca&lt;/a&gt;, a historical site.

Q: Are there any natural attractions like lakes or unique geological features to see?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its alabaster quarries, which have been active since Roman times. You can also find natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5146890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salada de Mediana Salt Flat&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2855635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galacho de La Alfranca Park&lt;/a&gt;. The Ebro river itself is a prominent natural feature, with trails often providing views of its course and surrounding orchards.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Fuentes De Ebro?
A: The trails in Fuentes De Ebro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from river plains and pine forests to the unique alabaster quarries, and appreciate the variety of options for different ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fuentes De Ebro?
A: Fuentes de Ebro, being in a semi-arid region, can experience hot summers. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, with milder weather and vibrant natural colors. Winter can also be suitable, though some areas might be cooler.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976173221/la-salada-de-la-mediana-and-the-birth-of-the-ginel-river-circular-from-mediana-de-aragon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Salada de la Mediana and the birth of the Ginel river — circular from Mediana de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 12.8-mile (20.7 km) experience, taking around 5 hours 26 minutes to complete.

Q: Which trails offer the best panoramic views of the region?
A: Many elevated points along the trails provide spectacular views. For instance, the 'Ruta de Valdepuy' ascends to higher ground, offering expansive vistas of olive groves, irrigated fields, and the Ginel river. From locations like Cabezo Pufarrando, you can enjoy panoramic views encompassing the orchards of Fuentes, the Ebro river, and the alabaster quarries.
